items.title: Resolution Rescinding Berkeley Police Department’s Pepper Spray Reporting
items.action: M/S/C (Lunaparra/Tregub) to suspend the rules and extend the meeting to 11:20pm. Vote: All Ayes. Action: 24 speakers. M/S/C (Ishii/Kesarwani) to adopt Resolution No. 72,159–N.S. as proposed by Mayor Ishii in Supplemental Communications Packet #2 and further amended at the meeting to read as below. RESOLUTION RESCINDING THE PEPPER SPRAY INCIDENT NOTIFICATION REQUIREMENT WHEREAS, in 1997 the City Council established a requirement for the Berkeley Police Department to notify Council each time pepper spray is used as a measure to bolster accountability; and WHEREAS, the separate notification process was introduced at a time when reporting mechanisms were limited and provided necessary oversight; and WHEREAS, the Berkeley Police Department has since implemented a comprehensive use- of-force reporting system that documents all related incidents, including those involving pepper spray in the Transparency Hub and the annual Use of Force report; and WHEREAS, until February 10, 2026, the Berkeley Police Department historically included pepper spray in the annual Controlled Equipment report; and WHEREAS, the Police Accountability Board submitted a recommendation to the City Council on February 23, 2026 that recommended reincorporating reporting on the use of pepper spray into the annual Controlled Equipment report to preserve key narrative details not otherwise available through BPD’s other reporting channels; and Tuesday, March 10, 2026 ANNOTATED AGENDA Page 12 WHEREAS, the administration of first aid was included in the original pepper spray use report that was transmitted to the Mayor and Council within 7 days; and WHEREAS, the continued requirement for separate notification directly to the Mayor and City Council creates redundant administrative tasks that strain City resources; and WHEREAS, rescinding the notification requirement will simplify reporting procedures and allow staff to concentrate on more critical operational matters while preserving public transparency. NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the City Council of the City of Berkeley that:

items.title: The requirement for the Berkeley Police Department to notify the City Council every time
All pepper spray incidents shall be recorded as part of the established use-of-force
Subsequent to the pepper spray report being signed off on by the Police Department
Refer to the City Manager to present an amendment to BMC Chapter 2.100 to the City
City of Berkeley’s 2026 State and Federal Legislative Platform (Continued from
items.action: Moved to Consent Calendar. Adopted Resolution No. 72,160–N.S. as revised in Supplemental Communications Packet #1 by Mayor Ishii and further amended at the meeting to include the language below. Support legislative and budgetary efforts to create a not-for-profit utility service to replace investor-owned utility programs, reduce the ratepayer burden imposed by investor-owned utilities, and support community-choice energy. (Infrastructure P olicy Priorities) Public Comment – Items Not Listed on the Agenda - 10 speakers. Adjournment Action: M/S/C (Taplin/Humbert) to adjourn the meeting. Vote: All Ayes. Adjourned at 11:00 p.m. Communications Item #17: Resolution Rescinding Berkeley Police Department’s Pepper Spray Reporting Requirement (Reviewed by the Public Safety Committee)
